name: Julep
what_it_does: Cloud platform for building stateful, long-running AI agents with built-in persistent memory, multi-step task execution, and tool integration — defined via YAML workflows.
use_cases: Orchestrate multi-day research workflows where an agent gathers, synthesizes, and reports on information across many sessions
Build customer support agents that maintain persistent conversation history and user preferences across interactions
Automate complex multi-step business processes (e.g., lead enrichment, outreach, follow-up) as durable agent tasks
Create agents that execute parallelized sub-tasks with conditional branching and error recovery without custom orchestration code
Deploy agents that integrate with external tools (email, calendar, APIs) within a managed execution environment
not_for: Simple single-turn LLM calls — the platform overhead is unnecessary for stateless request-response patterns
Agents requiring complete data isolation or on-premises deployment — Julep is a cloud-only managed platform
Teams needing Python-native agent logic with fine-grained control over every execution step — YAML workflows constrain flexibility
best_when: You need durable, resumable multi-step agent workflows with built-in memory and tool orchestration without building the infrastructure yourself.
avoid_when: Your agents are stateless, single-step, or require custom execution logic that YAML workflow definitions cannot express.

auth.notes: API key passed as Bearer token; key scoped per project

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier with limited executions and memory storage
pricing.paid_tiers: Usage-based paid tiers based on execution time and memory; contact for enterprise
pricing.notes: Freemium model; free tier suitable for development and small-scale agents; production workloads require paid plan

performance.rate_limits: Varies by plan; documented per tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Task execution errors are logged per step in execution history; error messages identify the failing step but can lack detail on root cause
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Task executions have unique IDs; re-triggering the same task creates a new execution — no built-in deduplication
known_agent_gotchas: YAML workflow definitions are expressive but have a learning curve — invalid YAML schema fails at execution start with error messages that reference internal schema fields
Long-running task executions are asynchronous — calling code must poll execution status or use webhooks; no blocking wait option
Memory and document store have size limits per agent — agents processing large document corpora may hit limits silently
Tool integrations require pre-registration in the Julep platform — agents cannot dynamically add tools at runtime
Session-level memory and agent-level memory have different scoping rules that are easy to confuse, leading to context leaking between users

security_score_components.security_notes: All agent memory and conversation data stored on Julep cloud infrastructure; no self-hosted option currently available
